one_time_description: Write down of investment in Treaty ($9,253,335) offset by Treaty loan payable forgiveness ($9,326,024), net positive $72,689
economic_notes: Band operates Kalum Ventures Ltd., Kalum Ventures Limited Partnership, Kalum Quarry Limited Partnership, Kitsumkalum Development Limited Partnership, Kalum Gas Bar Limited Partnership, and Kitsumkalum Economic Development Group Ltd. as government business enterprises. Band received Land Code during the year, transferring funds held in trust. Band entered Port of Prince Rupert Container Terminal Project Agreement.
notes: Audit opinion is qualified because the Band did not prepare an approved budget for the year ended March 31, 2020, as required by Canadian public sector accounting standards. This qualification also applied to the prior year (March 31, 2019). Consolidated subsidiaries include Kitsumkalum Treaty Administration, Kitsumkalum Social Housing, and House of Sim-Oi-Ghets. Six GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. Large one-time items: write-down of Treaty investment ($9,253,335) and Treaty loan forgiveness ($9,326,024) nearly offset each other. Prior year figures are restated per Note 22 (correction of an error). Transport Canada revenue of $4,600,000 is significantly higher than prior year $150,000, likely a one-time settlement. Province of BC revenue jumped from $1,218,852 to $16,516,495 — likely related to treaty/land title transactions.
